blocks.content:
Arrays have built-in methods to help you work with them.

## Common Array Methods

- `.push()` - Add item to the end
- `.pop()` - Remove item from the end
- `.shift()` - Remove item from the start
- `.unshift()` - Add item to the start
- `.includes()` - Check if array contains item
- `.indexOf()` - Find position of item
- `.join()` - Combine items into a string
- `.reverse()` - Flip the order
- `.sort()` - Sort items

blocks.title: Push and Pop
Shift and Unshift
Join and Reverse
Includes and IndexOf
blocks.code:
let colors = ['red', 'green', 'blue', 'yellow'];

console.log(colors.includes('blue'));     // true
console.log(colors.includes('purple'));   // false

console.log(colors.indexOf('green'));     // 1
console.log(colors.indexOf('red'));       // 0

questions.question: What does the push() method do?
questions.explanation: push() adds a new item to the end of an array.

questions.question: What does join() return?
questions.explanation: join() combines all array items into a single string with a separator.
